subject: Geography
title: Mumbai: City of Contrasts
description: Learn Mumbai: City of Contrasts in one minute.

hook.text: Mumbai generates 6% of India's entire GDP while packed onto a narrow peninsula.

buildup.text: Originally seven islands, landfill projects merged them into one dense urban mass.

discovery.text: Dharavi, one of Asia's largest slums, sits next to a billion-dollar financial district.

twist.text: Dharavi has its own thriving economy—recycling, pottery, and leather—worth over $1 billion a year.

climax.text: Mumbai proves that a megacity can be both enormously wealthy and deeply unequal.

punchline.text: Billionaires and slum dwellers share the same skyline.

quiz.question: What is Dharavi known for?
options: Being one of Asia's largest slums with a thriving economy
Having the tallest building in India
